publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-04-14 18:05:44 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, manuscripts, genealogical notes, clippings, and photographs.
did.abstract: Correspondence, manuscripts of articles, genealogical notes, clippings, and photographs pertaining to Reed's secretaryship of the Hobasco Masonic Lodge in Ithaca, New York; correspondence related to the early history of Cornell University, and the city of Ithaca; also, genealogical material pertaining to the Stevenson and Dumont families.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, manuscripts of articles, genealogical notes, clippings, and photographs pertaining to Reed's secretaryship of the Hobasco Masonic Lodge in Ithaca, New York; other correspondence pertains to the early history of Cornell University, and the city of Ithaca; also, genealogical material pertaining to the Stevenson and Dumont families.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Harry Clifford Reed papers, #1580. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed Mss. - 5 Copies

did.unittitle: "A Layman Looks at the Supreme Court"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of "The Winds" by Ellen Coit Elliott, inscribed to Reed.

did.unittitle: Series II. Genealogy: Notes and Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed notes and longhand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence (1950-1951) W. Willard James S. (1950); and notes. Pictures - people, houses, coat of arms. Letter - "Colonial records the Stevensons" (typescript of records found in the Land office, Annapolis, Maryland. Correspondence with cousin, Mrs. John Collins in Laurel, Delaware.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence (1950) and notes (T&H)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Picture of Reed - Reunion at Barton Hall - Duplicate in Box 6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History of Cornell University (buildings and people); "Cascadilla Place" - Mss. and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pictures of Reed at 1952 reunion, Mrs. Reed and daughter (?) (also negative), postcard photographs of scenes in Vermont (?).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from relatives and Reed

did.unittitle: Biographical Notes - Los Angeles from E. C. E.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class of '82 - employed in A. D. White's library next 2 years working on his projected catalogue of certain historical collections he had gathered in Europe. Lived in Cascadilla (1884-1891). Author of autobiography "It Happened This Way". Married in 1886 to O. L. Elliott, '85, aided David Starr Jordan in organization of Stanford. (original and typed copy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prof. George Sylvanus Moler - "One of the greatest minds in University plans to write biography. Prof. S. H. Gage cooperated with him in making tests of the light values of different lamp bulbs made by the Corning Company. Cornell - biographies "To me a University is not a campus with its buildings and lawns; it is the men who teach in it" - "mechanized education"

did.unittitle: Nivison - Notes from E. C. E.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cornell Faculty and University education in general.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wife died 1926; son - Bill; daughter - Margaret (Peggy): engineer - 10 years with Northwestern telephone Exchange Co. in Minneapolis. Father had clothing store in Ithaca. H. C. R. shared management thereafter, then sold out. Also mentioned elsewhere: Fairview Manor Realty Co., Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comments - refers to letter from Sam. Nivison to Cornell which makes C. appear a "very queer" light which he considers to be "entirely false"

did.unittitle: Notes from Judge Francis M. Finch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning her contemplated eye operation. Her family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Women at Cornell"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Comstocks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Praise for Ithaca, Comstocks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Gov. Warren

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Gov. Warren

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Burdett; Brown family; Woodward family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Sir Francis Burdette; Burdett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Woodward family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Family matters; Cascadilla

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Social Security, returned check - earned more than $50.

did.unittitle: Letter from F&T Jared Van Wagenen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Dumonds, Van Wagenen; Cornell - living expenses - biographies of faculty Boarding in 1887-91; Ithaca - 14 Linn St. - oldest structure in Tompkins County

did.unittitle: Letter from Frank R. C. Nivison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Has a picture of Miss Nivison taken about 1893. Reed: research for Cascadilla Place" Ms. and Miss Nivison - suggests picture be given to DeWitt Historical Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Question of whether Dumont, Yaple and Hinepaugh were first settlers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright of "Cascadilla Place" - USCO - forms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reed to N.Y. Genealogical and Biographical Society - Reed offers his material on Dumonts, Hinepaughs, Yaples, Woodworths and McDowells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inquiring about Somerset County Historical Quarterly which contains several articles on the Dumonts by John D. B. Vol. I, 1912

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcard - forwarding information

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Lou" in Binghamton to Reed - death of wife

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five letters exchanged between Lou and Reed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Reed to Howard T. Critchlow, Trenton, N.J. - requesting that he find out where the University Library might acquire a copy of "History of Somerset County, N.J.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reed to Brother Frank - Bills, receipts, withholding statement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Reed to Brink; Dumont, Brink and Jared van Wagenen to Reed - genealogy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Reed to Brink, Huybertse, Dumont - genealogy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Thank you's" lodge, nurses and doctors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List of states, electoral votes, how voted (D. or R.) (1952?); copy of letter written by T. B. Macaulay to Hon. Henry S. Randall (Secretary of State in N.Y. 1851-1853) re: Jeffersonian democracy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three typescript copies of Ms. Including 2 with corrections; notes (excerpts from various sources; copies of letters from Samantha Nivison to Cornell and other papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed and long hand notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buildings, notes from Ithaca Journal and other papers with items pertaining to Cornell University, Cascadilla Place and Women at Cornell, Miss Samantha Nivison, Crane and other faculty and student body.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Reed from Murray L. Richardson, Clerk of Orleans County - no record on a J. N. Greene of Medina, N.Y. who was supposed to have contracted to construct first College Building

did.unittitle: Ezra Cornell to Francis M. Finch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to and From Francis M. Finch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript article on Thomas Frederick Crane and excerpts from what others have written

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence concerning Wells College and "the Wells Female Dept. of the Cornell University"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bob married to Margaret, granddaughter of C.E.C. - forwarded his letter to C.E.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reactions to first letter from E.C.E. - "She must been astounding woman". Comments on Cornell - Thomas Dewey as possible President of Cornell, also administration of Day: teaching by graduate assistants; the idea of a "State University"

did.unittitle: Al, Sue & Thad Hoefer to Reed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed Christmas letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Speaks of recent illness

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labor Day 1952 - from Bob

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reed's health

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pending operation for cancer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lodge # 716, Ithaca - Reed Secretary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Form letter requesting Florida members to attend presentation of 50 Year Service Award to R. W. Blinn in Melbourne, Florida

did.unittitle: Handwritten notes and minutes of meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reed to Pittsburgh Lodge requesting a check on why a prospective member was rejected there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Pittsburgh: applicant has not changed his voting resident to Varna, still being considered in Pittsburgh

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Byron A. Schlag - Hobasco wants him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Florida Lodge - 2nd and 3rd degree

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To Florida Lodge - explanation of conduct of classes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence to a friend just restored to membership

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reed provides a list of some past Masters of Lodge and dates to Squier, Ithaca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Robert C. Kellogg, Master thanking a guest speaker in Aurora.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Bob - information for their use while he is in the hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Snapshot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph and negative of Mrs. Reed and daughter (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scenes in Vermont (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of old homes in Hurley and Kingston, N.Y.
